extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked with 1.6km remaining on the cobbled Osimo finish, holding off DUMOULIN Tom's late surge to win Stage 11
tactical_patterns: Androni-Sidermec deployed five riders to early break on Scarponi memorial stage, prioritizing visibility over GC defense.
Quick-Step rider initiated premature attack at 4.7km on cobbles, forcing Mitchelton-Scott to control rather than exploit numbers.
Dumoulin closed gap on flatter cobbled sections but lacked acceleration where gradient increased on technical surface combination.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: attacked at 1600m on steep cobbled ramp showing both power and tactical timing superior to pure time trialists
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: isolated chase effort suggests Sunweb lacked positioning entering cobbles despite this terrain theoretically suiting him
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: Lotto-Soudal positioned aggressively early on cobbles but faded when gradient steepened with 1400m remaining
rider_signals.MASNADA Fausto: positive: Androni rider willing to work in break despite no personal stage win chance, stayed committed until 5km
